name: Ermita Cristo de Cabrera y Castillo de Santa Cruz Navagallega – circular desde Frades de la Sierra
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Frades De La Sierra traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and open countryside. The region features varied terrain, including paths leading to historical sites and recreational areas. With elevations reaching up to 1465 meters at Cervero Peak, the area offers routes with significant climbs and descents. Best mountain bike trails around Frades De La Sierra The most popular mountain bike trail is Ermita Cristo de Cabrera y Castillo de Santa Cruz Navagallega – circular desde Frades de la Sierra, a 22.1 miles (35.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete, offering a journey through historical sites and scenic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cervero Peak – Pico Cervero (1465 m) loop from Monleón, a moderate 24.0 miles (38.7 km) path, featuring challenging climbs to a prominent peak with expansive vistas. Local mountain bikers also love the La Honfría Recreation Area loop from Herguijuela del Campo, a 23.1 miles (37.1 km) trail leading through recreational forest areas, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Mountain biking around Frades De La Sierra is defined by rolling hills, forested sections, and open country paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on moderate mountain bike trails. The routes in Frades De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Frades De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Frades De La Sierra?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails around Frades De La Sierra listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for riders.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Frades De La Sierra?
A: The mountain bike trails in Frades De La Sierra are predominantly of &lt;strong&gt;moderate&lt;/strong&gt; difficulty. Out of 11 available routes, 10 are rated moderate, with one easy option, making the area suitable for riders with some experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Frades De La Sierra?
A: Yes, many of the trails are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994086014/ermita-cristo-de-cabrera-y-castillo-de-santa-cruz-navagallega-circular-desde-frades-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Cristo de Cabrera y Castillo de Santa Cruz Navagallega – circular desde Frades de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 22.1-mile loop, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31514488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervero Peak – Pico Cervero (1465 m) loop from Monleón&lt;/a&gt; for a more challenging 24.0-mile ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The region features varied terrain, including rolling hills, dense woodlands, and open countryside. You'll encounter paths leading to historical sites and recreational areas, with routes offering significant climbs and descents, especially towards Cervero Peak.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Frades De La Sierra?
A: While most trails are moderate, there is one easy mountain bike route available, which could be suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. Always check the specific route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I find along the mountain bike trails?
A: You can discover several points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1491056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Frades de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3804910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago Cross on Pico de Las Dueñas&lt;/a&gt; is a notable summit. Some routes also pass through settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5445838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monterrubio de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Frades De La Sierra?
A: The region's climate generally allows for mountain biking through much of the year. Spring and autumn often provide the most pleasant temperatures and conditions. Summer can be warm, while winter rides are possible but may require checking local conditions for frost or rain.

Q: Are there any facilities or places to stop near the trails?
A: Yes, you might find facilities along or near some routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3803461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue Parroquial Santa María&lt;/a&gt; is listed as a facility in the area, offering a potential stop for riders.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Frades De La Sierra?
A: The mountain bike trails in Frades De La Sierra are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the journey through historical sites, and the expansive vistas from peaks like Cervero.

Q: Are there any less-known or 'hidden gem' mountain bike trails?
A: For a route that explores recreational forest areas, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31508765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Honfría Recreation Area loop from Herguijuela del Campo&lt;/a&gt;. It's a 23.1-mile moderate trail that offers a different perspective of the region's natural beauty.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: While many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always best to check local regulations or specific trail rules regarding dogs, especially in protected areas or private lands. Ensure your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash where required.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Frades De La Sierra?
A: Specific parking areas are not detailed for each route, but trailheads often have designated or informal parking spots. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for user-generated information or local signage regarding parking availability.

A beautiful, very quiet area that can be the ideal place to take a break on your route and enjoy this beautiful landscape.

The castle ruins are of great historical and tourist interest in the Peña de Santa Cruz area and also offer beautiful views. The origin of it is unknown for sure, but it is considered to be from pre-Roman times, and which later formed part of a larger group to defend the lands at the beginning of the 10th century and which was rebuilt in this century by King Ramiro II of León .
